topic: Summerlin West Handyman: High-Ticket Repair Packages for Sun-Blasted Exteriors, Trim, and Finish Work
excerpt: Summerlin sits on the west side of Las Vegas near Red Rock, and that west-valley exposure matters because the broader Las Vegas climate brings abundant sunshine, triple-digit summer heat, and monsoon-season storm swings that are rough on caulk, paint, door hardware, and outdoor wood. Underneath, the valley’s native soils are shallow, well-drained alluvial soils with carbonates/caliche, so even small movement can telegraph as sticking doors, recurring wall cracks, or gates that go just a little out of square. In Summerlin West, that usually shows up on the exterior first: dried-out trim joints, patchy stucco touchups, gate sag, and wall repairs that look fine until late-afternoon light hits them sideways. Summer is the punishing season here, then storm bursts come through and reveal the weak seams—so the durable fix is usually a high-ticket package that handles alignment, sealing, finish blending, and water-path correction together instead of piecemeal.
markdown_tables:
| High-ticket Summerlin West package | What it usually includes | Representative total |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Exterior trim + seal reset | failed caulk lines, sun-baked trim repairs, prime/seal, paint-ready finish | $2,500–$8,500 |
| Multi-room wall and ceiling finish package | recurring cracks, wide floats, texture matching, halo cleanup | $2,000–$9,000 |
| Gate, fence, and hardware rebuild scope | sagging gates, post/rail corrections, latch alignment, hardware reset | $2,000–$8,500 |
| Water-control and storm-prep package | gutter corners, discharge fixes, splash-back corrections, exterior reseals | $2,500–$9,500 |
| Pre-listing west-side curb appeal package | exterior sightline fixes, doors, trim, finish cleanup, second-pass punch | $3,500–$12,500 |
| Outdoor living refresh bundle | deck/fence staining prep, exterior hardware, trim and surface repairs | $2,500–$10,000 |
